items.title: Warriors rookie Yaxel Lendeborg named Summer League MVP, All Summer-League teams announced
items.summary: Yaxel Lendeborg was one of the breakout stars of Summer League. He looked like a hand-in-glove fit for the Warriors, capped off by him dropping 21 and 10 in the championship game to lead Golden State to the Summer League crown.Yaxel Lendeborg, the 11th overall pick, wins the 2026 NBA Summer League Championship Game MVP! 21 PTS 10 REB 2 STL WARRIORS W pic.twitter.com/bSa4XbBBi2— NBA (@NBA) July 20, 2026That capped off a run that saw Lendeborg named Las Vegas Summer League MVP. Over the course of six games he averaged 14.8 points, 6.8 rebounds and four assists per game.The NBA also released the All-Summer-League teams:First TeamCameron Boozer (Memphis)Brayden Burries (Milwaukee)Yaxel Lendeborg (Golden State)Meleek Thomas (Cleveland)Caleb Wilson (Chicago)Second TeamCedric Coward (Memphis)Egor Demin (Brooklyn)Khaman Maluach (Phoenix)Liam McNeeley (Charlotte)Zyon Pullin (Minnesota)It's…

items.title: Stephen Curry becomes first current player to receive exhibit at the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame
items.summary: Golden State Warriors legend Stephen Curry isn't eligible for the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame just yet … but he's going to be honored by the establishment anyway. Curry will become the first-ever current player to receive his own exhibit at the Hall of Fame, it announced Tuesday. The exhibit, titled "Stephen Curry: Beyond the Arc," will focus on — of course — Curry's prowess from 3-point land. Fans hoping to see the exhibit won't have to wait long, as it opens July 24.He inspired a generation to dream bigger and shoot farther. Now, for the first time in its nearly 75-year history, the Hall of Fame will feature a dedicated experience honoring an active NBA player. “Stephen Curry: Beyond the Arc” - a limited time only exhibit opens Friday,… pic.twitter.com/7jbbhYc0x5— Basketball HOF (@Hoophall) July 21, 2026The exhibit is quite the honor, as no other current…

items.title: Yaxel Lendeborg named MVP, Cameron Boozer, Caleb Wilson make NBA All-Summer League first team
items.summary: On the heels of a loaded NBA Draft, there was heightened interest in this year's Summer League. It didn't disappoint. Some of the top names from June's draft put up tantalizing performances in Las Vegas, and the NBA honored them Tuesday by announcing an MVP and All-Summer League Teams.Yaxel Lendeborg (14.8 ppg, 6.8 rpg, 4 apg, 2 spg, 35.7% from 3), who was named MVP of the championship game after leading the Golden State Warriors past Cameron Boozer and the Memphis Grizzlies, earned top honors as MVP of the entire league. Lendeborg outplayed Boozer down the stretch of a tight title game to secure the win and MVP honors. He earned the hardware months after helping lead Michigan to an NCAA championship.Boozer (18.5 ppg, 8 rpg, 3.7 apg, 1.8 spg, 33.3% from 3), who delivered with consistent production throughout the league, was named to the All-Summer League first team. As was…

items.title: Hawks trade Zaccharie Risacher, best free agents available + New York Liberty check-in
items.summary: Subscribe to The Dunker SpotApple Podcasts | Spotify | YouTubeWe have a fun episode of The Dunker Spot coming your way!First, Nekias Duncan and Steve Jones catch up on the latest transaction news in the NBA, headlined by the three-team deal that sent former #1 pick Zaccharie Risacher to the Dallas Mavericks and Lu Dort to the Atlanta Hawks. They then discuss the most interesting free agents available and their ideal fits.On the WNBA side, the guys discuss the historic pace the Indiana Fever's offense is on, then have a nuanced conversation about the New York Liberty. How much of the Liberty's season is salvageable?They close with an All-Star discussion: grading the draft between Team Coop and Team Spoon, sharing the lineups and matchups they're most excited about, and debating who should be the Kelsey Plum replacement.If you ever have NBA or WNBA questions, email us at…

items.title: Why Hugo Gonzalez has potential to be Derrick White-level player
items.summary: Why Hugo Gonzalez has potential to be Derrick White-level player originally appeared on NBC Sports BostonYears ago, Celtics insider Chris Forsberg learned from Kyrie Irving (by way of the ever-so-wise Brad Stevens) a simple truth about life: Comparison is the thief of joy.That may be true, but when it comes to projecting ceilings for young players, comparisons are often the clearest way to try to envision what the future may hold. And in the case of Hugo Gonzalez — Boston’s selection at No. 28 overall in the 2025 draft — the comparisons and projections are starting to fly.On the latest edition of the Celtics Talk Podcast, Forsberg and SI’s John Karalis focused on the 20-year-old Gonzalez after his performances at Summer League and FIBA qualifying competition, with Karalis using Derrick White as a potential comparison.“I think he could be one of your top-three guys, and I don’t think…

items.title: When will LeBron James announce his decision? 'We're not going to be rushed,' agent Rich Paul said
items.summary: If it feels like the LeBron James free agency saga has dragged on and on, it's because it has. Free agency officially opened more than three weeks ago (June 30), and you can be sure LeBron's agent, Rich Paul, had conversations with other teams before that (the NBA is a tampering league).So when will his decision come? Paul would not give a timeline, speaking on the latest edition of his “Game Over” podcast with Max Kellerman (released Monday night)."We're not going to be rushed. We're not going to be rushed. It's his choice to make, and when he makes the choice, he'll make it..."I don't know when his choice is going to be made, but it could be 48 seconds. It could be 48 minutes. I don't know. Again, you can't make somebody know something that they don't know."Paul also pushed back on the idea that LeBron was in no hurry because he…

items.title: Jalen Brunson says Knicks have not talked about whether team will attend White House after championship
items.summary: New York Knicks owner James Dolan gladly accepted an offer to go to the White House to celebrate his team's championship, but it's unclear who he'll have with him. Star guard Jalen Brunson said Monday that the team has yet to discuss the issue, telling Variety, "We'll cross our bridge when we get to it."NBA teams have — famously, in some cases — not celebrated their championships at the White House with President Donald Trump in office. The Golden State Warriors started that trend in 2017, when Stephen Curry and Trump got into a feud, resulting in Trump revoking the team's invite. Since then, no NBA team has celebrated their championship at the White House with Trump. During Joe Biden's tenure as president, three NBA teams attended the White House for a championship celebration.Brunson didn't rule out the idea to Variety, simply saying the Knicks…

items.title: How would Warriors fans feel about adding LeBron James
items.summary: NEW YORK, NEW YORK - JULY 16: LeBron James speaks onstage during the TIME100 Sports Gala at Pier 60, Chelsea Piers on July 16, 2026 in New York City. (Photo by Craig Barritt/Getty Images for TIME) | Getty Images for TIME While reports continue to suggest that LeBron James is favoring a return to the Eastern Conference, the Golden State Warriors continue to be mentioned with an outside shot of landing King James. As James allows his free agency saga to continue deeper and deeper into the offseason, in his quintessential melodramatic fashion, fanbases around the league are dealing with a Schrödinger’s roster, one with and without James at once. A recent poll of Dub Nation from SB Nation Reacts asked Warriors fans how they would feel about signing James, given the team’s prolonged rivalry with the former Cavs, Heat, and Lakers star.Perhaps unsurprisingly for those around the fanbase, the…

items.title: Jalen Wilson to Hawks, Ziaire Williams with Lakers as Tyson Etienne, Trevon Scott go overseas
items.summary: Over the past few days, several players from the Brooklyn Nets’ 2025-26 squad have found new homes, specifically forwards Jalen Wilson, Ziaire Williams, two-way guard Tyson Etienne, and Trevon Scott, a forward who had two 10-day contracts with the team to close out the year.It is of course not unusual for a team in the rebuild phase following a season where the team won 20 games and had an overhaul that included three new draft picks, a big trade and a couple of free agent signings.According to HoopsHype’s Michael Scotto, Wilson is the latest. He is signing a two-way contract with the Atlanta Hawks for the 2026-27 season after his team option was declined by Brooklyn earlier this offseason.The Atlanta Hawks and Jalen Wilson have agreed to a two-way deal, league sources told @hoopshype. Wilson averaged 7.4 points per game in three seasons with the Brooklyn Nets.…
